disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Well-demarcated dyspigmented upper-truncal patches with fine branny (dust-like) scale on stretch/scratch, worse after heat/humidity
trigger_question_examples: Do the patches show a fine dusty scale when you stretch or lightly scratch the skin, and are they on the upper trunk?
Did they flare after hot, humid, sweaty conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, a fine dusty scale appears when I stretch or scratch them, they're on my upper chest/back, and they flared after the tropical heat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pale patches have surface scale (not the completely smooth, scale-free pale patches of a pigment-loss condition)
trigger_question_examples: Are the pale patches completely smooth like normal skin, or do they have a fine surface scale?
Is the colour totally white/chalky or off-white with scale?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They have a fine surface scale — they're not completely smooth or chalk-white.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Palms/soles clear, no mouth sores or swollen glands (secondary-syphilis screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any involvement of your palms/soles, mouth sores, or swollen glands?
Are your palms, soles or mouth affected at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my palms/soles are clear, no mouth sores or swollen glands, and I feel well.
